instruction: Deploy WordPress on my OneKE cluster with a dedicated namespace and persistent storage
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy WordPress on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "10 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - wordpress
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create wordpress namespace"
    params:
      name: wordpress
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for WordPress data"
    params:
      name: wordpress-data
      namespace: wordpress
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy WordPress via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: wordpress
      namespace: wordpress
      release_name: wordpress
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: wordpress-data
        service:
          type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for WordPress pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: wordpress
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=wordpress"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the WordPress access URL"
    params:
      service_name: wordpress
      namespace: wordpress
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify WordPress pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove WordPress Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: wordpress
        namespace: wordpress
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the WordPress PVC"
      params:
        name: wordpress-data
        namespace: wordpress
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the wordpress namespace"
      params:
        name: wordpress

instruction: Remove the Redis deployment from the production namespace on my OneKE cluster
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Remove Redis deployment from production namespace"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "3 minutes"
  risk_level: high
  tags:
    - redis
    - cleanup
    - production
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.get_status
    description: "Check current Redis deployment status before removal"
    params:
      release_name: redis
      namespace: production
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.uninstall
    description: "Uninstall Redis Helm release"
    params:
      release_name: redis
      namespace: production
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    on_failure: abort
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is reachable"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify production namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Redis pods are terminated"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.deploy
      description: "Re-deploy Redis if removal was unintended"
      params:
        chart: redis
        namespace: production
        release_name: redis
        repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

instruction: Scale my OneKE cluster to 5 worker nodes
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Scale OneKE cluster to 5 worker nodes"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - scaling
    - cluster
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.cluster.get_status
    description: "Check current cluster status before scaling"
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.cluster.list_nodes
    description: "List current nodes to verify baseline"
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.cluster.scale_nodes
    description: "Scale cluster to 5 worker nodes"
    params:
      worker_count: 5
    depends_on:
      - step_02
    timeout_seconds: 600
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is healthy and reachable"
  post_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is still healthy after scaling"

instruction: Create a namespace called monitoring, deploy Prometheus with a 50Gi volume, and expose its web UI
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up Prometheus monitoring with dedicated namespace, storage, and external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "8 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - monitoring
    - prometheus
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create monitoring namespace"
    params:
      name: monitoring
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Prometheus data"
    params:
      name: prometheus-data
      namespace: monitoring
      size: 50Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Prometheus via Helm"
    params:
      chart: prometheus
      namespace: monitoring
      release_name: prometheus
      repo_url: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
      values:
        server:
          persistentVolume:
            existingClaim: prometheus-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Prometheus to become ready"
    params:
      namespace: monitoring
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Prometheus web UI via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: prometheus-server
      namespace: monitoring
      port: 9090
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Prometheus is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify Prometheus service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Prometheus"
      params:
        release_name: prometheus
        namespace: monitoring
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Prometheus storage"
      params:
        name: prometheus-data
        namespace: monitoring
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove monitoring namespace"
      params:
        name: monitoring

instruction: Power off virtual machine 42 and take a snapshot before the maintenance window
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Power off VM 42 and create a pre-maintenance snapshot"
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - vm
    - maintenance
    - snapshot
    - opennebula
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: one.vm.poweroff
    description: "Power off the VM gracefully"
    params:
      vm_id: 42
    timeout_seconds: 120
  - id: step_02
    action: one.vm.snapshot_create
    description: "Create a pre-maintenance snapshot"
    params:
      vm_id: 42
      snapshot_name: pre-maintenance-snapshot
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    timeout_seconds: 300
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: vm_exists
      description: "Verify VM 42 exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: vm_exists
      description: "Verify snapshot was created"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: one.vm.resume
      description: "Resume the VM if snapshot failed"
      params:
        vm_id: 42

instruction: Set up a MongoDB deployment on my OneKE cluster with a new namespace and claims for storage
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up MongoDB on OneKE cluster with namespace and storage claims"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "8 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - mongodb
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create mongodb namespace"
    params:
      name: mongodb
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for MongoDB data"
    params:
      name: mongodb-data
      namespace: mongodb
      size: 20Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy MongoDB via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: mongodb
      namespace: mongodb
      release_name: mongodb
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: mongodb-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for MongoDB pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: mongodb
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=mongodb"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get MongoDB access URL"
    params:
      service_name: mongodb
      namespace: mongodb
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify MongoDB pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove MongoDB Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: mongodb
        namespace: mongodb
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the MongoDB PVC"
      params:
        name: mongodb-data
        namespace: mongodb
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the mongodb namespace"
      params:
        name: mongodb

steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create rabbitmq namespace"
    params:
      name: rabbitmq
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for RabbitMQ data"
    params:
      name: rabbitmq-data
      namespace: rabbitmq
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy RabbitMQ via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: rabbitmq
      namespace: rabbitmq
      release_name: rabbitmq
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: rabbitmq-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for RabbitMQ pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: rabbitmq
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=rabbitmq"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get RabbitMQ access URL"
    params:
      service_name: rabbitmq
      namespace: rabbitmq
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify RabbitMQ pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove RabbitMQ Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: rabbitmq
        namespace: rabbitmq
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the RabbitMQ PVC"
      params:
        name: rabbitmq-data
        namespace: rabbitmq
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the rabbitmq namespace"
      params:
        name: rabbitmq

instruction: Delete the MongoDB application from the dev namespace on the OneKE cluster.

version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Remove MongoDB deployment from dev namespace"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "4 minutes"
  risk_level: high
  tags:
    - mongodb
    - cleanup
    - development
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.get_status
    description: "Check current MongoDB deployment status before removal"
    params:
      release_name: mongodb
      namespace: dev
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.uninstall
    description: "Uninstall MongoDB Helm release"
    params:
      release_name: mongodb
      namespace: dev
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    on_failure: abort
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is reachable"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify dev namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify MongoDB pods are terminated"
rollback:

    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.deploy
      description: "Re-deploy MongoDB if removal was unintended"
      params:
        chart: mongodb
        namespace: dev
        release_name: mongodb
        repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

metadata:
  description: "Remove Kafka deployment from message-broker namespace"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "3 minutes"
  risk_level: high
  tags:
    - kafka
    - cleanup
    - messaging
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.get_status
    description: "Check current Kafka deployment status before removal"
    params:
      release_name: kafka
      namespace: message-broker
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.uninstall
    description: "Uninstall Kafka Helm release"
    params:
      release_name: kafka
      namespace: message-broker
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    on_failure: abort
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is reachable"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify message-broker namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Kafka pods are terminated"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.deploy
      description: "Re-deploy Kafka if removal was unintended"
      params:
        chart: kafka
        namespace: message-broker
        release_name: kafka
        repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

instruction: Increase the number of worker nodes in my OneKE cluster to 5.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Increase worker nodes in OneKE cluster to 5"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - scaling
    - cluster
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.cluster.get_status
    description: "Check current cluster status before scaling"
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.cluster.list_nodes
    description: "List current nodes to verify baseline"
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.cluster.scale_nodes
    description: "Scale cluster to 5 worker nodes"
    params:
      worker_count: 5
    depends_on:
      - step_02
    timeout_seconds: 600
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is healthy and reachable"
  post_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is still healthy after scaling"

instruction: Set up a namespace named metrics, deploy Grafana with a 20Gi volume, and make its dashboard accessible.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Establish Grafana metrics visualization with dedicated namespace, storage, and public access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "7 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - monitoring
    - grafana
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create metrics namespace"
    params:
      name: metrics
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Grafana"
    params:
      name: grafana-data
      namespace: metrics
      size: 20Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana via Helm"
    params:
      chart: grafana
      namespace: metrics
      release_name: grafana
      repo_url: https://grafana.org/helm-charts
      values:
        persistence:
          enabled: true
          existingClaim: grafana-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: metrics
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Grafana dashboard via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: grafana
      namespace: metrics
      port: 3000
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Grafana is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify Grafana service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Grafana"
      params:
        release_name: grafana
        namespace: metrics
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Grafana storage"
      params:
        name: grafana-data
        namespace: metrics
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove metrics namespace"
      params:
        name: metrics

instruction: As a manager, please create a namespace called production, deploy PostgreSQL with a 45Gi volume, and expose its service.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up PostgreSQL in a dedicated production namespace with public access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "9 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - database
    - postgres
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create production namespace"
    params:
      name: production
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for PostgreSQL"
    params:
      name: postgres-data
      namespace: production
      size: 45Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy PostgreSQL via Helm"
    params:
      chart: postgresql
      namespace: production
      release_name: postgres
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: postgres-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for PostgreSQL to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: production
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=postgresql"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose PostgreSQL service"
    params:
      deployment_name: postgres
      namespace: production
      port: 5432
      service_type: ClusterIP
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify PostgreSQL is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify PostgreSQL service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove PostgreSQL"
      params:
        release_name: postgres
        namespace: production
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete PostgreSQL storage"
      params:
        name: postgres-data
        namespace: production
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove production namespace"
      params:
        name: production

instruction: Shut down virtual machine with ID 42 and create a snapshot before working on it.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Shut down VM 42 and create a snapshot before updates"
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - vm
    - shutdown
    - snapshot
    - opennebula
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: one.vm.poweroff
    description: "Gracefully shut down the VM"
    params:
      vm_id: 42
    timeout_seconds: 120
  - id: step_02
    action: one.vm.snapshot_create
    description: "Create a snapshot before VM updates"
    params:
      vm_id: 42
      snapshot_name: pre-update-snapshot
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    timeout_seconds: 300
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: vm_exists
      description: "Check if VM 42 is running"
  post_checks:
    - type: vm_exists
      description: "Ensure snapshot was successfully created"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: one.vm.resume
      description: "Resume VM if snapshot creation fails"
      params:
        vm_id: 42

instruction: Set up a MongoDB instance in a new namespace on my OneKE cluster with persistent storage.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy MongoDB on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - mongodb
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create mongodb namespace"
    params:
      name: mongodb
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for MongoDB data"
    params:
      name: mongodb-data
      namespace: mongodb
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy MongoDB via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: mongodb
      namespace: mongodb
      release_name: mongodb
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: mongodb-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for MongoDB pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: mongodb
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=mongodb"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the MongoDB access URL"
    params:
      service_name: mongodb
      namespace: mongodb
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify MongoDB pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove MongoDB Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: mongodb
        namespace: mongodb
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the MongoDB PVC"
      params:
        name: mongodb-data
        namespace: mongodb
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the mongodb namespace"
      params:
        name: mongodb

      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=jenkins"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the Jenkins access URL"
    params:
      service_name: jenkins
      namespace: jenkins
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Jenkins pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Jenkins Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: jenkins
        namespace: jenkins
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the Jenkins PVC"
      params:
        name: jenkins-data
        namespace: jenkins
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the jenkins namespace"
      params:
        name: jenkins

steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.upgrade
    description: "Upgrade the application to the latest version."
    params:
      chart: "my-app-chart"
      release_name: "my-app"
      namespace: "default"
      version: "2.0"
    on_failure: continue
    timeout_seconds: 300
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify the namespace exists before upgrading."
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Ensure pods are running after upgrade."
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.upgrade
      description: "Rollback to the previous version of the application."
      params:
        chart: "my-app-chart"
        release_name: "my-app"
        namespace: "default"
        version: "1.0"

instruction: I need to set up a namespace named alerts, deploy Grafana, and make its UI accessible.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Establish Grafana for alerts with a unique namespace and external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "7 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - alerts
    - grafana
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create alerts namespace"
    params:
      name: alerts
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Grafana"
    params:
      name: grafana-storage
      namespace: alerts
      size: 30Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana using Helm"
    params:
      chart: grafana
      namespace: alerts
      release_name: grafana
      repo_url: https://grafana.github.io/helm-charts
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: grafana-storage
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: alerts
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Grafana UI via LoadBalancer"
    params:
      deployment_name: grafana
      namespace: alerts
      port: 3000
      service_type: LoadBalancer
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Check cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Ensure Grafana is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Check Grafana service is reachable"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Grafana"
      params:
        release_name: grafana
        namespace: alerts
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Grafana storage"
      params:
        name: grafana-storage
        namespace: alerts
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete alerts namespace"
      params:
        name: alerts

instruction: As a developer, could you create a namespace called logging, deploy ELK stack, and expose Kibana?
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy ELK stack with logging namespace and available Kibana access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "10 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - logging
    - elk
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create logging namespace"
    params:
      name: logging
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for ELK data"
    params:
      name: elk-data
      namespace: logging
      size: 100Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy ELK stack using Helm"
    params:
      chart: elasticsearch
      namespace: logging
      release_name: elk
      repo_url: https://elastic.github.io/helm-charts
      values:
        persistence:
          enabled: true
          existingClaim: elk-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for ELK stack to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: logging
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=elasticsearch"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Kibana UI via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: kibana
      namespace: logging
      port: 5601
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is reachable"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Make sure ELK pods are running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Ensure Kibana service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove ELK stack"
      params:
        release_name: elk
        namespace: logging
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete ELK storage"
      params:
        name: elk-data
        namespace: logging
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove logging namespace"
      params:
        name: logging

instruction: Set up a WordPress application with persistent storage and expose it to the internet.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up a WordPress application with persistent storage and expose it to the internet."
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create a namespace for the WordPress application."
    params:
      name: "wordpress"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create a persistent volume claim for WordPress data."
    params:
      name: "wordpress-pvc"
      namespace: "wordpress"
      size: "10Gi"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy WordPress application with the PVC."
    params:
      chart: "bitnami/wordpress"
      namespace: "wordpress"
      release_name: "wordpress"
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: "wordpress-pvc"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose the WordPress application to the internet."
    params:
      namespace: "wordpress"
      deployment_name: "wordpress"
      port: 80
      service_type: "LoadBalancer"
    depends_on: [step_03]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if the namespace 'wordpress' is available."
  post_checks:
    - type: service_available
      description: "Check if the WordPress service is available."
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete the WordPress namespace upon rollback."
      params:
        name: "wordpress"

instruction: Create a staging environment, deploy an application, and verify its health.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create a staging environment, deploy an application, and verify its health."
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create a staging namespace."
    params:
      name: "staging"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy the application in the staging namespace."
    params:
      chart: "my-app"
      namespace: "staging"
      release_name: "my-app-staging"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for the application to be ready."
    params:
      namespace: "staging"
      label_selector: "app=my-app"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if the namespace 'staging' is available."
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Check if the application pods are running."
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete the staging namespace upon rollback."
      params:

instruction: Scale the cluster, deploy a new version of an app, and check its status.

metadata:

      namespace: "production"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: continue
validation:

  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running

rollback:

    - id: step_90

      params:

instruction: Set up a MongoDB database in a new namespace on my OneKE cluster, ensuring it has persistent storage.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up MongoDB on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "8 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - mongodb
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create mongodb namespace"
    params:
      name: mongodb
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for MongoDB data"
    params:
      name: mongodb-data
      namespace: mongodb
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy MongoDB via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: mongodb
      namespace: mongodb
      release_name: mongodb
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: mongodb-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for MongoDB pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: mongodb
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=mongodb"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the MongoDB access URL"
    params:
      service_name: mongodb
      namespace: mongodb
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify MongoDB pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove MongoDB Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: mongodb
        namespace: mongodb
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the MongoDB PVC"
      params:
        name: mongodb-data
        namespace: mongodb
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the mongodb namespace"
      params:
        name: mongodb

instruction: Could you set up a namespace named alerts, deploy Grafana with a 20Gi volume, and make its dashboard accessible?
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Establish Grafana for alert monitoring with dedicated namespace, storage, and external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "7 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - alerts
    - grafana
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create alerts namespace"
    params:
      name: alerts
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Allocate persistent volume for Grafana data"
    params:
      name: grafana-storage
      namespace: alerts
      size: 20Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana using Helm"
    params:
      chart: grafana
      namespace: alerts
      release_name: grafana
      repo_url: https://grafana.github.io/helm-charts
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: grafana-storage
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana to become ready"
    params:
      namespace: alerts
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Grafana dashboard via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: grafana
      namespace: alerts
      port: 3000
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Check cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Ensure Grafana is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Confirm Grafana service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Grafana"
      params:
        release_name: grafana
        namespace: alerts
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Grafana storage"
      params:
        name: grafana-storage
        namespace: alerts
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove alerts namespace"
      params:
        name: alerts

instruction: Set up a monitoring stack with Prometheus and Grafana in a new namespace
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up a monitoring stack with Prometheus and Grafana in a new namespace"
  target_cluster: "OneKE"
  estimated_duration: "10 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create monitoring namespace"
    params:
      name: "monitoring"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Prometheus"
    params:
      chart: "prometheus"
      namespace: "monitoring"
      release_name: "prometheus"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana"
    params:
      chart: "grafana"
      namespace: "monitoring"
      release_name: "grafana"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: "monitoring"
      label_selector: "app=grafana"
    depends_on: [step_03]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if namespace 'monitoring' is available"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Check if Grafana is running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete monitoring namespace"
      params:
        name: "monitoring"

instruction: Create a VM, set up a namespace, and deploy a web app with persistent storage
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create a VM, set up a namespace, and deploy a web app with persistent storage"
  target_cluster: "OneKE"
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: one.vm.create
    description: "Create a VM for web app"
    params:
      template_id: 101
      name: "web-app-vm"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create namespace for web app"
    params:
      name: "webapp"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for web app"
    params:
      name: "webapp-pvc"
      namespace: "webapp"
      size: "5Gi"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy web app"
    params:
      chart: "my-web-app"
      namespace: "webapp"
      release_name: "web-app"
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: "webapp-pvc"
    depends_on: [step_03]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for the web app to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: "webapp"
      label_selector: "app=my-web-app"
    depends_on: [step_04]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: resource_available
      description: "Check if resources are available for VM creation"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Check if web app pod is running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete web app namespace"
      params:
        name: "webapp"
    - id: step_91
      action: one.vm.delete
      description: "Delete the web app VM"
      params:
        vm_id: 101

instruction: Scale up the cluster workers, deploy a new app version, and verify it's healthy
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Scale up the cluster workers, deploy a new app version, and verify it's healthy"
  target_cluster: "OneKE"
  estimated_duration: "20 minutes"
  risk_level: high
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.cluster.scale_nodes
    description: "Scale up cluster workers"
    params:
      worker_count: 5
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.upgrade
    description: "Upgrade web app to new version"
    params:
      chart: "my-web-app"
      release_name: "web-app"
      namespace: "webapp"
      version: "2.0"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for upgraded web app to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: "webapp"
      label_selector: "app=my-web-app"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Check if existing pods are running"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Ensure upgraded web app pod is running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.upgrade
      description: "Rollback web app to previous version"
      params:
        chart: "my-web-app"
        release_name: "web-app"
        namespace: "webapp"
        version: "1.0"

instruction: Deploy Nginx using Helm on the default namespace
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy Nginx on the default namespace using Helm"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: low
  tags:
    - nginx
    - helm
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Nginx via Helm chart to default namespace"
    params:
      chart: nginx
      namespace: default
      release_name: nginx
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      create_namespace: false

instruction: Set up a MongoDB instance on my OneKE cluster with its own namespace and storage
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy MongoDB on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - mongodb
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create mongodb namespace"
    params:
      name: mongodb
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for MongoDB data"
    params:
      name: mongodb-data
      namespace: mongodb
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy MongoDB via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: mongodb
      namespace: mongodb
      release_name: mongodb
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: mongodb-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for MongoDB pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: mongodb
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=mongodb"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the MongoDB access URL"
    params:
      service_name: mongodb
      namespace: mongodb
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify MongoDB pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove MongoDB Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: mongodb
        namespace: mongodb
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the MongoDB PVC"
      params:
        name: mongodb-data
        namespace: mongodb
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the mongodb namespace"
      params:
        name: mongodb

instruction: I need to set up a new namespace named alerts, deploy Grafana with a 20Gi volume, and make its dashboard accessible.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up Grafana for monitoring with dedicated namespace, storage, and external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "7 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - monitoring
    - grafana
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create alerts namespace"
    params:
      name: alerts
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Grafana data"
    params:
      name: grafana-data
      namespace: alerts
      size: 20Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana via Helm"
    params:
      chart: grafana
      namespace: alerts
      release_name: grafana
      repo_url: https://grafana.github.io/helm-charts
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: grafana-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana to become ready"
    params:
      namespace: alerts
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Grafana dashboard via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: grafana
      namespace: alerts
      port: 3000
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Grafana is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify Grafana service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Grafana"
      params:
        release_name: grafana
        namespace: alerts
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Grafana storage"
      params:
        name: grafana-data
        namespace: alerts
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove alerts namespace"
      params:
        name: alerts

instruction: As a developer, can you create a namespace called dev-env, deploy Redis with a 10Gi volume, and allow access to its service?
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:

  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "6 minutes"

      namespace: dev-env
      size: 10Gi

instruction: Set up Grafana in the monitoring namespace with Helm
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy Grafana in the monitoring namespace using Helm"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: low
  tags:
    - grafana
    - helm
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana via Helm chart to monitoring namespace"
    params:
      chart: grafana
      namespace: monitoring
      release_name: grafana
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      create_namespace: false
    timeout_seconds: 300
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: monitoring
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the Grafana service endpoint"
    params:
      service_name: grafana
      namespace: monitoring
    depends_on:
      - step_02
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify monitoring namespace is accessible"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Grafana pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Grafana Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: grafana
        namespace: monitoring

  description: "Install PostgreSQL in existing database namespace"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - postgresql
    - database
    - helm
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy PostgreSQL via Helm to database namespace"
    params:
      chart: postgresql
      namespace: database
      release_name: postgresql
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      create_namespace: false
      values:
        postgresqlPassword: password123
    timeout_seconds: 300
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for PostgreSQL pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: database
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=postgresql"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_01
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify database namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify PostgreSQL pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove PostgreSQL Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: postgresql
        namespace: database

instruction: Set up Redis in the existing data namespace and expose it on port 6379
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy Redis to existing data namespace with external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"

    - redis
    - data
    - helm
    - oneke

      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      create_namespace: false

      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:

    - type: cluster_reachable

    - type: namespace_exists

    - type: pods_running

rollback:

      params:
        release_name: redis
        namespace: data

instruction: Set up a monitoring stack with Prometheus and Grafana on the OneKE cluster.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up a monitoring stack with Prometheus and Grafana"
  target_cluster: "OneKE"
  estimated_duration: "10 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create monitoring namespace"
    params:
      name: "monitoring"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Prometheus in the monitoring namespace"
    params:
      chart: "prometheus"
      namespace: "monitoring"
      release_name: "prometheus"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana in the monitoring namespace"
    params:
      chart: "grafana"
      namespace: "monitoring"
      release_name: "grafana"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if the monitoring namespace is available"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Ensure Prometheus and Grafana pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Uninstall Grafana"
      params:
        release_name: "grafana"
        namespace: "monitoring"
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Uninstall Prometheus"
      params:
        release_name: "prometheus"
        namespace: "monitoring"
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete monitoring namespace"
      params:
        name: "monitoring"

instruction: Create a staging namespace, deploy the app with persistent storage, and expose it.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create a staging namespace, deploy app with persistent storage, and expose it"
  target_cluster: "OneKE"
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create staging namespace"
    params:
      name: "staging"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for app"
    params:
      name: "app-pvc"
      namespace: "staging"
      size: "5Gi"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy application in the staging namespace"
    params:
      chart: "myapp"
      namespace: "staging"
      release_name: "myapp"
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: "app-pvc"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose the application service"
    params:
      namespace: "staging"
      deployment_name: "myapp"
      port: 80
      service_type: "LoadBalancer"
    depends_on: [step_03]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if the staging namespace is available"
  post_checks:
    - type: service_available
      description: "Check if the app service is available"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
      description: "Remove service for the app"
      params:
        namespace: "staging"
        service_name: "myapp"
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Uninstall the application"
      params:
        release_name: "myapp"
        namespace: "staging"
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete persistent volume claim"
      params:
        name: "app-pvc"
        namespace: "staging"
    - id: step_93
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete staging namespace"
      params:
        name: "staging"

instruction: Create a VM, set up a namespace for it, and deploy an application inside the namespace.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create a VM, set up a namespace, and deploy an application"
  target_cluster: "OneKE"
  estimated_duration: "25 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: one.vm.create
    description: "Create a VM for the application"
    params:
      template_id: 1
      name: "app-vm"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create a namespace for the application"
    params:
      name: "app-namespace"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy the application in the new namespace"
    params:
      chart: "myapp"
      namespace: "app-namespace"
      release_name: "myapp"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: vm_exists
      description: "Check if the VM was created successfully"
  post_checks:
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Ensure the namespace was created"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Uninstall the application"
      params:
        release_name: "myapp"
        namespace: "app-namespace"
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete the application namespace"
      params:
        name: "app-namespace"
    - id: step_92
      action: one.vm.delete
      description: "Delete the VM"
      params:
        vm_id: 1

instruction: Create a persistent volume, deploy a database, and expose it.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create a persistent volume, deploy a database, and expose it"
  target_cluster: "OneKE"
  estimated_duration: "18 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create a persistent volume claim for the database"
    params:
      name: "db-pvc"
      namespace: "database"
      size: "10Gi"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy the database in the database namespace"
    params:
      chart: "mysql"
      namespace: "database"
      release_name: "mysql"
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: "db-pvc"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose the database service"
    params:
      namespace: "database"
      deployment_name: "mysql"
      port: 3306
      service_type: "ClusterIP"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if the database namespace is available"
  post_checks:
    - type: service_available
      description: "Check if the database service is available"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
      description: "Remove database service"
      params:
        namespace: "database"
        service_name: "mysql"
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Uninstall the database"
      params:
        release_name: "mysql"
        namespace: "database"
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete persistent volume claim"
      params:
        name: "db-pvc"
        namespace: "database"

instruction: Scale down the database cluster and upgrade the app in production.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Scale down the database cluster and upgrade the application in production"
  target_cluster: "OneKE"
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.cluster.scale_nodes
    description: "Scale down the database cluster nodes"
    params:
      worker_count: 2
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.upgrade
    description: "Upgrade the application in the production namespace"
    params:
      chart: "myapp"
      release_name: "myapp"
      namespace: "production"
      version: "3.0"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for the upgraded application to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: "production"
      label_selector: "app=myapp"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Check if the cluster is reachable"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Ensure the upgraded app pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.upgrade
      description: "Rollback the application to the previous version"
      params:
        chart: "myapp"
        release_name: "myapp"
        namespace: "production"
        version: "2.0"
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.cluster.scale_nodes
      description: "Scale back up the database cluster nodes"
      params:
        worker_count: 4

instruction: Scale down worker nodes, create a new namespace, and deploy an admin panel.
